memory_seeds.text: I served in the Reach defense force during the border skirmishes. Lost my squad to a Compact ambush that intelligence swore wasn't there. The sensors were wrong. They're always wrong when you need them most.

I don't trust scanners. I trust patterns. A patrol route that repeats is a trap. A faction that never shows hostility is waiting. The Loup-Garou is flying through the most patrolled space in the sector — every run is a gamble.
The captain asked me why I always sit with my back to the wall. I told them it's because I've never lost a fight I saw coming.
